Title: Enhancing melatonin biosynthesis in crops through synthetic genetic circuits: A strategy for nutritional fortification in soybean and stress resistance in cotton

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis-derived activation domain with bacterial DNA-binding module
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Glycine max; Gossypium hirsutum
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Synthetic transcriptional regulator composed of AmtR DNA-binding protein, ERF2 activation domain and SV40 NLS
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed as the synthetic activator component of BUFFER circuits
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: G1090 constitutive promoter for activator expression; napin promoter used in soybean NPSC context for seed-focused module deployment
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Synthetic operator sequences upstream of target gene cassette
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary supplement for human health; improves plant salinity tolerance and resistance to Verticillium dahliae
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables precise, orthogonal, threshold-dependent activation of COMT/SNAT modules, thereby enhancing melatonin synthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A synthetic activator composed of AmtR, ERF2 activation domain and SV40 NLS was built to drive orthogonal plant genetic circuits. 2. BUFFER gates with different operator copy numbers tuned COMT expression strength. 3. This circuit architecture enabled quantitative control of melatonin production in transient leaves and stable crop transformants.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synthetic circuit construction; transient expression; qPCR; metabolite quantification
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Platform component for orthogonal metabolic engineering in crops beyond melatonin
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a synthetic regulator rather than a native nutrient-pathway gene; included because it is a core experimentally manipulated determinant of melatonin output.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Glycine max; Gossypium hirsutum
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Caffeic acid O-methyltransferase / N-acetylserotonin O-methyltransferase-like COMT
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final methylation step of melatonin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary supplement for human health; improves plant salinity tolerance and resistance to Verticillium dahliae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; seeds; soy milk after processing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; predominantly expressed in developing soybean seeds under napin promoter; expressed in cotton leaves and seeds under constitutive circuit
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Minimal CaMV 35S target promoter activated by synthetic AmtR-ERF2-NLS BUFFER circuits; napin promoter for soybean seed-specific expression; G1090 constitutive promoter for synthetic activator
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM NaCl during soybean germination; Verticillium dahliae infection in cotton; post-harvest storage and soy milk processing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melatonin accumulation in leaves and seeds; contributes to improved soybean salt tolerance during germination and enhanced cotton resistance to V. dahliae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Codon-optimized COMT from Gossypium hirsutum was a core engineered melatonin-pathway gene in synthetic BUFFER circuits. 2. COMT expression increased in N. benthamiana under BUFFER circuits, with associated melatonin elevation. 3. Seed-specific COMT co-expression with SNAT in soybean increased seed melatonin up to 31-fold in line NPSC-14 without yield penalty. 4. Constitutive COMT co-expression with SNAT in cotton increased endogenous melatonin and improved resistance to V. dahliae.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; terminal-step gene used as a key engineering lever
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; stable transformation; qPCR; UPLC-MS/MS; ELISA; metabolite profiling; stress/disease phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Non-targeted metabolomics by UPLC-MS/MS; supplementary data only, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal-step engineering gene for melatonin biofortification and stress-resilient crop design; suitable for stacking with SNAT in synthetic circuits and seed-specific deployment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with increased protein and reduced oil in soybean; shorter plants with shortened internodes in cotton under constitutive expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats COMT as a pivotal melatonin biosynthetic gene and uses codon-optimized sequence based on G. hirsutum COMT; TPH was noted as not identified in plants.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Glycine max; Gossypium hirsutum
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates serotonin to form N-acetylserotonin, the immediate precursor of melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary supplement for human health; improves plant salinity tolerance and resistance to Verticillium dahliae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; seeds; soy milk after processing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with COMT in 3XSC/NPSC circuits; SNAT expression in soybean tissues shown in Figure S2, predominantly seed-associated under napin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Minimal CaMV 35S target promoter activated by synthetic AmtR-ERF2-NLS BUFFER circuits; napin promoter for soybean seed-specific expression; G1090 constitutive promoter for synthetic activator
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM NaCl during soybean germination; Verticillium dahliae infection in cotton; post-harvest storage and soy milk processing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melatonin biosynthesis when stacked with COMT, increasing melatonin in transient leaves, soybean seeds, and cotton tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Codon-optimized SNAT from Chlamydomonas reinhardtii was combined with COMT in a dual-gene synthetic circuit. 2. The 3XSC construct produced the highest melatonin among tested BUFFER circuits in N. benthamiana. 3. Seed-specific SNAT+COMT expression in soybean strongly elevated seed melatonin and retained melatonin after storage and soy milk processing. 4. SNAT+COMT expression in cotton elevated endogenous melatonin and increased resistance to V. dahliae.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; pathway-stacking gene improving flux to melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; stable transformation; qPCR; UPLC-MS/MS; ELISA; metabolite profiling; stress/disease phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Non-targeted metabolomics by UPLC-MS/MS; supplementary data only, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable partner gene for COMT stacking in melatonin biofortification and stress-resistance engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In soybean lines expressing SNAT with COMT, protein increased and oil decreased; in cotton constitutive lines, shorter stature occurred
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Codon-optimized sequence was based on Chlamydomonas reinhardtii SNAT; pathway diagram places SNAT activity in chloroplasts.
